name: Alto del Escamplero, San Juan de la Arena y Avilés – circular desde Oviedo
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Oviède are characterized by a diverse landscape featuring rugged mountains, lush forests, and river valleys. The region offers significant elevation gains, including legendary ascents, alongside more gentle, scenic paths. Limestone cliffs and rolling hills define much of the terrain, providing varied challenges for road cyclists. Best road cycling routes around Oviède The most popular road cycling route is Alto del Escamplero, San Juan de la Arena y Avilés – circular desde Oviedo, a 61.6 miles (99.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 47 minutes to complete. This difficult route features substantial climbs and coastal views. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is La Vega – View of Monsacro from Angliru loop from Llamaquique, a difficult 39.5 miles (63.5 km) path. This route includes significant elevation gain, traversing mountainous terrain with panoramic vistas. Local road cyclists also love the Les Campes Square – Good area for riding on flat terrain loop from La Corredoria, a 22.4 miles (36.0 km) trail leading through varied terrain with minimal elevation, often completed in about 1 hour 35 minutes. Road cycling around Oviède is defined by rugged mountains, river valleys, and challenging ascents. The network offers options for all ability levels, from easy greenways to demanding climbs. The routes in Oviède are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 9,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Oviède's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Oviedo?
A: Oviedo offers a diverse selection of road cycling routes, with over 140 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 11 easy, 65 moderate, and 70 difficult trails.

Q: Are there any beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Oviedo?
A: Yes, Oviedo has routes suitable for beginners. For a relatively flat and accessible ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15035441" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Les Campes Square – Good area for riding on flat terrain loop from La Corredoria&lt;/a&gt;. This easy 22.4-mile (36.0 km) trail has minimal elevation gain, making it a great option for those new to road cycling in the area.

Q: What kind of challenging climbs can I find for road cycling near Oviedo?
A: Oviedo is renowned for its challenging ascents, including legendary climbs that have featured in the Vuelta a España. Cyclists can test their endurance on iconic routes like the Alto de L'Angliru and Gamoniteiro. For a difficult route with significant elevation, try the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20500832" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Vega – View of Monsacro from Angliru loop from Llamaquique&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 39.5 miles (63.5 km) with substantial climbing.

Q: Are there any scenic or natural attractions to see along the road cycling routes?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in natural beauty. You can cycle through lush forests, alongside river valleys, and past rugged mountains. Some routes offer views of the Nora River meanders. For specific natural features, consider exploring highlights like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/297709" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Route of the Xanas&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2949141" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Desfiladero de Peñas Juntas on the Senda del Oso&lt;/a&gt;, which are part of the stunning gorges around Oviedo.

Q: Can I find any circular road cycling routes around Oviedo?
A: Yes, many road cycling routes in Oviedo are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069438741/alto-del-escamplero-san-juan-de-la-arena-y-aviles-circular-desde-oviedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Escamplero, San Juan de la Arena y Avilés – circular desde Oviedo&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 61.6-mile (99.2 km) route offering coastal views and substantial climbs.

Q: What is the best time of year for road biking in Oviedo?
A: The region, often called 'Green Spain,' benefits from a cool and moist climate. While specific 'best' times can depend on personal preference for weather, the lush landscapes are enjoyable throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures for cycling, avoiding the peak heat of summer or potential winter chill in higher elevations.

Q: Are there any routes that combine road cycling with views of the coast?
A: Yes, Oviedo's strategic location means the coast is within reach. Routes can take you towards vibrant coastal towns like Gijón and Avilés, offering views of the Bay of Biscay. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1069438741/alto-del-escamplero-san-juan-de-la-arena-y-aviles-circular-desde-oviedo" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto del Escamplero, San Juan de la Arena y Avilés – circular desde Oviedo&lt;/a&gt; route, for instance, includes coastal views as part of its challenging terrain.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Oviedo?
A: The road cycling routes in Oviedo are highly regarded by the komoot community, holding an average rating of 4.4 stars from over 140 reviews. More than 9,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore the varied terrain, often praising the diverse landscapes, from rugged mountains to scenic river valleys, and the range of challenges available.

Q: Are there any routes that pass through historical or cultural landmarks?
A: While the focus is on road cycling, Oviedo itself is rich in history, featuring an 8th-century cathedral and UNESCO World Heritage-listed monuments like Santa María del Naranco. Many routes start or pass near the city, allowing you to combine your ride with exploring these cultural highlights. The region's natural features, like the Nora River meanders, also hold historical significance.

Q: Can I find routes that are less crowded for a more peaceful ride?
A: While some popular routes like the Senda del Oso (a Greenway, not strictly road cycling but accessible) can see more traffic, the extensive network of over 140 routes means you can often find quieter roads, especially on lesser-known loops or during off-peak times. Exploring routes slightly further from the immediate city center can also lead to more serene cycling experiences.

Q: Are there any long-distance road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, Oviedo offers several longer routes for endurance cyclists. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31915264" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alto de La Campa – Alto de la Madera loop from Parque Principado&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult route spanning over 64 miles (103 km) with significant elevation gain, providing a substantial challenge for experienced riders.
tips: Popular climb from Oviedo to Escamplero. El Escamplero is an important place in Asturian history, as it is a transit area for the Camino de Santiago and the old royal road of Galicia. The climb is 3.7 km with a maximum slope of almost 14%.

The Castillo de San Martín, in the council of Soto del Barco, is located on the right bank of the river Nalón, on a pronounced bend from which the channel opens towards the estuary of San Esteban de Pravia. Its location, on a promontory barely 40 m high, provides it with an excellent visual domain over the mouth of the most important river artery in the region and justifies its long history as a fortification.
The current castle was built by Alfonso III on the remains of a previous settlement, in order to protect the coast and the estuary from Norman incursions.

A golden cove in the heart of the tourist resort
The beach of Santa Maria del Mar, in the municipality of Castrillón, is a well-equipped urban cove that has a high occupancy rate. Just 300 meters long, it opens at the mouth of the Ferrera River.
Close to two campsites, it is bordered by the village of Santa Maria del Mar. Note an islet on its right, La Ladrona (the thief), whose legend has it that it was inhabited by a giant squid.
https://www.turismoasturias.es/fr/descubre/costa/playas/playa-de-santa-maria-del-mar
